title: ficus infectoria var. fraseri
intro: Ficus infectoria var. fraseri, also known as Indian rubber or bayur tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive rubbery sap and medicinal properties. Traditionally, the sap has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which have been linked to various health benefits, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Ficus infectoria var. fraseri typically involve the sap, which can be extracted and used in topical applications, such as creams or ointments, or ingested in capsule form after being diluted with other ingredients. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Ficus infectoria var. fraseri, individuals may be able to alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
